title: Orders, Payment Escape Hatch, And Refund Requests
purpose: Teach the agent how to inspect orders and create refund requests with the current V3 CLI package.
when_to_use: The user wants to inspect one order.
The agent needs to list orders for an account-scoped bearer session.
The user wants to request a refund.
The checkout page is unavailable and the operator needs the payment-intent escape hatch.

needs: order_id for order or refund flows

commands.intent: read one canonical order by id
commands.success_signal: order details and a status hint are printed
orders are listed, or the CLI prints that no orders were found

commands.intent: create a payment intent only when the normal checkout page cannot do it
commands.success_signal: payment_intent_id and current payment state are printed

commands.intent: request a refund for an order
commands.success_signal: refund request id and refund status are printed

agent_rules: Use itpay order when you already know the exact order id.
Use itpay orders only with an account-scoped bearer token. Missing or wrong scope should be surfaced as an error, not guessed away.
Treat itpay pay as an operator escape hatch. The checkout handoff remains the preferred path.
Never ask the user or client to supply refund payment ids, amount, currency, buyer id, or actor; the backend derives them from the owned order.
The refund response is the current source of truth; do not assume success locally.
forbidden: Do not create a payment intent first if normal checkout rendering is available.
Do not claim a refund succeeded before the backend response says so.

next_docs.condition: Need host-specific render behavior for checkout handoff
